title: Angel question
wikitext:
[]: Angel question             Quote Post by Gsnake18 » Sun Jul 05, 2009 10:11 pm If 2 Angels came in contact with eachother, do u think they would fight eachother or team up somehow. Just something I've wondered.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jul 05, 2009 10:30 pm Only one lifeform can evade destruction and seize the day. So, I would imagine if two angels were both trying to unite with Adam, that they would fight each other for the prize. Still, it's speculative.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Mon Jul 06, 2009 12:20 am it'd be a race.

[]:             Quote Post by JCunningham02 » Mon Jul 06, 2009 12:30 am I'm guessing they would team up, seeing as how they all came from the same life form and are technically part of the same race.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Mon Jul 06, 2009 12:53 am Can multiple Angels unite with Adam?

[]:             Quote Post by Gsnake18 » Mon Jul 06, 2009 1:49 am I wanna say no, but that's just a guess they very well could, but it never happens so who knows.

[]:             Quote Post by i_Love_Asuka » Mon Jul 06, 2009 2:16 am i honestly think they would fight each other, but first they unite to beat the crap out of the Lilins and then beat the crap out of each other for the prize like one guy above me said. other theory: they would fight each other cause one Angel doesn't know the other Angel is an Angel and vica versa. cause angels know no shit 'bout what's going on on earth. i refer to Kaworu, when he reached the central dogma and he saw Lilith it freaked him out cause he was expecting Adam. shame you have such a bad intel-section when you have Divine Powers...

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Mon Jul 06, 2009 5:10 am i_Love_Asuka wrote: i refer to Kaworu, when he reached the central dogma and he saw Lilith it freaked him out cause he was expecting Adam.

[]:             Quote Post by i_Love_Asuka » Mon Jul 06, 2009 5:17 am master_lloyd wrote: i_Love_Asuka wrote: i refer to Kaworu, when he reached the central dogma and he saw Lilith it freaked him out cause he was expecting Adam.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Mon Jul 06, 2009 5:34 am It's not my interpretation. They flat out tell him where Adam is. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by i_Love_Asuka » Mon Jul 06, 2009 5:50 am master_lloyd wrote: It's not my interpretation. They flat out tell him where Adam is. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Jul 06, 2009 9:55 am Well the Angel's wanna boff Lilith and end the world. I guess it just depends on how open certain Angels are to a Menage a trois. For some reason I can see Bardiel would be open to the idea (and I know Kaworu would as long as Shinji's involved) but some Angels like Ramiel and Zeurel strike me as too close minded for that. ....Work is slow....

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jul 06, 2009 10:19 am JCunningham02 wrote: I'm guessing they would team up, seeing as how they all came from the same life form and are technically part of the same race.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Mon Jul 06, 2009 11:31 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Only one lifeform can evade destruction and seize the day.

[]:             Quote Post by i_Love_Asuka » Mon Jul 06, 2009 3:28 pm so, there's that scene! thanks for pointing me towards it Reichu! i didn't had the luck to see the directors cut yet, but i will change that soon! (didn't knew that the freakin' plot is different in the DC)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jul 07, 2009 2:32 am @VoidEater: The "Angel" designations are difficult to force into something completely logical. I'm not sure I would read too much into the segregation of Adam's children into separate "Angels". In Misato's #25' expositional speech, she groups them together as a race of humans opposed to us, which flies in the face of the strangely persistent idea that the Angels were "incompatible" with not only us but each other as well. As for why Angels never worked together and all that jive... http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=6668 ( Or go straight to Reichu brainwashing post )

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Tue Jul 07, 2009 2:04 pm ^ I understand where you're coming from, but have my doubts. My context is that the Angels are hatching in sequence because only one is "allowed" or needed at a time. That Angel imagines itself into a form that may work well with the planet, and if it fails, then the next one tries things out. I realize this has logical issues with a planet like ours where an Angel can fill a particular niche (ocean, land...lava...) and leave room for others. But there are likely planets where this kind of variety may not obtain, and the FAR may have planned for them as the norm. The exposition about the Angels being complete is meaningful to me in that it precludes a need for others to accomplish anything or experience wholeness. I think this is a theme in NGE, and speaks to the psychological, social and metaphysical implications of having a hole in your heart (loneliness). The theme gets dilluted for me without underscoring it in this way - you only need one Angel on the planet for Adamic "success". Given the known context of Angels (they are complete, they show up one at a time, they seem to almost get "better" in some ways over time) tells us they are effective as individuals and might be presenting serial evolution. Given their psychological context, the concept of cooperation doesn't occur. It's like an adult with certain social disabilities. If you see yourself as independent, stand-alone, self-sufficient, complete unto yourself, the social interaction that would lead to the concept of cooperation to accomplish bigger/better things doesn't generate. With this in mind, NGE suggests our incomplete, lonely nature is a mechanism toward "Lilim" success. If the exposition about an Angel reaching Adam puts the kibosh on Lilim, it could also retard the development of any remaining Adamic larva. If this is as we're told, the whole Angelic race to Adam seems set up for a single individual winner. A race of Angels can still be in opposition to the race of Lilim, collectively - but I perceive them as serial individuals, each representing a race but each working as a legion unto itself.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jul 07, 2009 7:19 pm VoidEater wrote: The exposition about the Angels being complete

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Tue Jul 07, 2009 10:20 pm They would merge together and make a Super Angel but they have to use the fusion dance made famous by Dragon Ball Z

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Wed Jul 08, 2009 3:49 pm Reichu wrote: Out of curiosity, what is the source for this? I can't recall anything about "complete"ness offhand. (And Angels do feel loneliness, in the event you were implying otherwise.)
